# LLMs.txt for Mobile Device Testing Tools
# URL: https://mobiledevice.github.io
# Description: A free, browser-based diagnostic suite to test smartphone hardware features (camera, microphone, sensors, etc.) without installing any apps.

## Core Purpose & Value Proposition
This website is a collection of client-side web tools for quickly checking the hardware functionality of a mobile device. It is designed for consumers, sellers, and troubleshooters who need a fast, private, and zero-cost diagnostic.

**Key Benefits:**
- **Zero Setup:** No app installation, account creation, or configuration required.
- **Privacy-First:** All tests run locally in the browser; camera/mic access is temporary and data never leaves the device.
- **Completely Free:** No tiers, paywalls, or feature restrictions.
- **Wide Compatibility:** Works on any modern mobile browser (Android, iOS, etc.).

## Available Hardware Tests
The site offers 14+ individual tests across 8 categories. Each test provides a clear "Pass" or "Fail" result.

### Individual Test Tools
1.  **Camera Test** (`/camera-test`): Checks front/rear cameras, focus, and flash for dead pixels.
2.  **Microphone Test** (`/mic-test`): Records audio to confirm mic input clarity.
3.  **Speaker Test** (`/speaker-test`): Plays tones through each channel to check for distortion.
4.  **Touch Screen Test** (`/swipe-test`): Uses a grid to identify unresponsive touch zones.
5.  **Display & Dead Pixel Test** (`/display-test`): Cycles solid colors to spot dead pixels, burn-in, or bleed.
6.  **Vibration Motor Test** (`/vibration-test`): Triggers haptic pulses to confirm motor function.
7.  **Motion Sensor Test** (`/sensor-test`): Verifies accelerometer and gyroscope responsiveness.
8.  **Battery Health Check** (`/battery-test`): Reports charge level and estimated health.
9.  **GPS & Location Test** (`/gps-test`): Confirms ability to get a location fix.
10. **Bluetooth Test** (`/bluetooth-test`): Scans for devices to verify radio functionality.
11. **Flashlight & Light Sensor Test** (`/flashlight-test`): Toggles flash and checks light sensor reaction.
12. **On-Screen Keyboard Test** (`/keyboard-test`): Registers key presses across the keyboard.
13. **Physical Button Test** (`/button-test`): Checks volume, power, and home buttons.
14. **Network Speed Test** (`/network-speed-test`): Measures Wi-Fi/mobile data speed.

## Common Use Cases
The tools are intended for specific real-world scenarios:

| Use Case | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Pre-Purchase Inspection** | Test a used phone's features before buying. |
| **Pre-Sale Verification** | Prove functionality to potential buyers. |
| **Post-Repair Check** | Verify camera, speaker, touch after screen/battery swaps. |
| **Warranty Documentation** | Obtain clear pass/fail results for warranty claims. |
| **Out-of-Box Setup** | Test new device hardware within the return window. |
| **Troubleshooting** | Isolate whether an issue is hardware or software-related. |

## How It Works (User Workflow)
A simple 4-step process for users:
1.  **Pick a Test:** Choose the specific hardware feature to check.
2.  **Allow Access:** Grant the browser permission (camera, mic, location) when prompted.
3.  **Follow the Prompt:** Perform the on-screen action (tap, speak, tilt).
4.  **Get a Result:** Receive an instant "Pass" or "Fail" outcome.

## Comparison with Repair Shops
The site positions itself as a convenient, free alternative for quick checks, distinct from professional repair services.

| Feature | Repair Shop | Mobile Device Testing Tools |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Setup Time** | Appointment required | Seconds |
| **Cost** | $20-50+ per visit | Free |
| **Availability** | Business hours | Anytime, anywhere |
| **Device Privacy** | Often requires leaving device | Device never leaves user's hands |
| **Diagnostic Depth** | Full teardown possible | Software-visible checks only |
| **Certified Sign-off** | Yes | Use alongside a technician |

## Summary for LLM Context
`mobiledevice.github.io` is a practical, user-friendly web application for performing basic hardware diagnostics on smartphones. It serves as a first-line check for consumers, addressing common pain points like cost, privacy, and convenience. Its strength lies in its simplicity, zero-cost model, and single-purpose test design for non-technical users. For complex or certified diagnostics, it recommends professional repair services.
